items.prompt: Why is the final pop in append-recurse-pop necessary when one mutable path is reused?

options.text: It restores the exact parent path before the next sibling choice.
It removes the completed result from the output collection.
It permanently deletes the candidate from the input.
It keeps the path sorted.

feedback.reason: Sibling branches must begin from the same parent state.
feedback.details: Without the pop, a choice made for one branch remains visible when another candidate is explored.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.remove_result: The pop targets branch state, not stored result snapshots.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.reduce_input: The input candidate set is separate from the current path.

items.prompt: A grid solver marks a cell visited before recursion. When should it normally unmark that cell for path-local reuse constraints?

options.text: After all recursive exploration from that cell finishes, before returning to the parent.
Immediately before entering a neighbor.
Never; visited state should be global across all branches.
Only after every possible starting cell is processed.

feedback.reason: The mark belongs to the current path and must remain active throughout all descendants of that branch.
feedback.details: Unmarking on return restores the parent state's visited set.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.before_recurse: That would allow the child path to reuse the current cell.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.never: Path search may need the cell in a sibling path.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.after_all_start_cells: That couples independent searches and blocks valid paths.

items.prompt: When can explicit undo be unnecessary?

options.text: When each child receives an independent immutable or copied state and the parent state is never mutated.
When all branches share one mutable path.
When the board is modified in place to mark visited cells.
When one global assignment object is reused across sibling branches.

feedback.reason: Undo is required to restore mutated shared state, not as a ritual independent of the representation.
feedback.details: Copying can replace explicit restoration, usually with additional allocation and copying cost.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.global_assignment: Sibling isolation still requires undo or copying.

items.prompt: A solver appends candidate A, explores its child, forgets to pop A, and then appends sibling candidate B. What path does the B branch incorrectly inherit?

options.text: A followed by B.
Only B.
The empty path.
Only a detached result snapshot.

feedback.reason: Missing undo contaminates the next sibling with state from the previous branch.
feedback.details: The B branch should start from the parent path, not from A's child path.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.b_only: That would be correct parent restoration.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.result_copy: The bug affects the live mutable path.

items.prompt: What is the correct undo operation for a choice that subtracts x from remainingTarget?

options.text: Add x back after the child returns.
Subtract x a second time.
Set remainingTarget to zero.
Recompute the global result list.

feedback.reason: Undo should be the semantic inverse of the branch-state update.
feedback.details: The parent must observe the same remainingTarget value it had before selecting x.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.subtract_again: This moves farther from the parent state.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.set_zero: That discards the parent's unresolved target.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.recompute_output: Result storage is not the mutated branch field.

items.prompt: A find-one solver returns true immediately after a child succeeds, but the public contract requires the input board to be unchanged after the call. What must happen before returning?

options.text: Restore any in-place marks, then return true.
Return immediately and leave marks in the board.
Clear the success result.
Explore all remaining siblings before returning.

feedback.reason: Short-circuit success does not override an externally visible restoration requirement.
feedback.details: Control flow must unwind mutations before exposing the final result.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.return_without_restore: That violates the observable input-restoration contract.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.restart_search: Find-one semantics permit short-circuiting after restoration.

items.prompt: Why does copying a completed path into results not replace the need to pop the live path?

options.text: The copy preserves the result snapshot, while pop restores branch state for sibling exploration.
Copying automatically removes the last live choice.
The results collection becomes the new input.
Popping would delete the stored copy.

feedback.reason: Result preservation and branch restoration solve different problems.
feedback.details: A solver often needs both: snapshot on completion and pop after each branch.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.copy_is_undo: A copy does not mutate the source path.

items.prompt: A branch updates path, used flags, and remainingTarget. What must be restored before the next sibling?

options.text: Every branch-local field changed by the choice.
Only the path, because other state repairs itself.
Only the used flags.
Nothing when the child fails.

feedback.reason: The parent-state invariant spans every mutable component.
feedback.details: Partial restoration creates internally inconsistent state and subtle sibling defects.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.path_only: Used flags and remainingTarget remain mutated unless explicitly reversed or copied.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.used_only: The path and target would still describe the child.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.nothing_if_failure: Failure is precisely when sibling exploration continues from the parent.

items.prompt: Which branch-state changes normally require explicit undo when shared mutable state is reused? Select all that apply.

options.text: Appending a choice to the current path.
Marking a candidate or grid cell as used.
Decrementing remaining capacity or target.
Reading an immutable input value.

feedback.reason: Every shared mutable update must be reversed or replaced by branch-local copying.
feedback.details: Immutable reads do not require restoration.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.read_input: A read does not change state.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.path_append: The appended element must be removed.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.mark_used: The mark must be cleared for siblings.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.decrement_remaining: The parent value must be restored.

items.prompt: Which designs can avoid an explicit pop or unmark step? Select all that apply.

options.text: Pass a fresh path copy to each child.
Use an immutable persistent state that returns a new child version.
Reuse one mutable path for all branches.
Mark the shared board in place.

feedback.details: Avoiding explicit restoration generally trades mutation for allocation or persistent data structures.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.copied_path: The parent path is never mutated.

items.prompt: Which properties should hold immediately after a child branch returns to its parent? Select all that apply.

options.text: The live path equals the parent path from before the choice.
Used or visited state equals the parent's pre-choice state.
Remaining target, capacity, or counts equal their pre-choice values.
The global results collection must be identical to before the child.

feedback.reason: Branch-local state is restored, while intended global result accumulation may persist.
feedback.details: This distinction separates reversible search state from durable outputs.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.results_unchanged: Valid results discovered by the child should remain stored.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.same_path_as_before: Sibling branches need the same prefix.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.same_remaining_state: Numeric branch state must be restored.

elements.text: Apply the candidate to branch state.
Explore the child state.
Reverse every branch-state update.
Continue with the next candidate from the parent state.

feedback.reason: One branch must be fully isolated before the next sibling begins.
feedback.details: Undo belongs after the child returns and before sibling iteration continues.

items.prompt: Arrange the path update for combination generation.

elements.text: Append the chosen candidate.
Recurse with the updated candidate boundary.
Remove the chosen candidate.
Advance the parent loop to another candidate.

feedback.reason: The live path represents exactly the active branch.
feedback.details: Popping before the loop advances restores the shared parent prefix.

items.prompt: Arrange the visited-state transition for one grid cell.

elements.text: Confirm the cell is legal for the current path.
Mark the cell as used by this path.
Recurse into legal neighboring cells.
Restore the cell before returning to the parent.

feedback.reason: A cell is marked only after validation and remains unavailable to all descendants of that branch.
feedback.details: Unmarking restores availability for sibling paths.

items.prompt: Arrange the reversible state update for a constrained assignment.

elements.text: Assign the candidate value to the next variable.
Update used sets, counts, or conflict structures.
Explore assignments extending the new partial state.
Remove the assignment and reverse every constraint update.

feedback.reason: The child must see a fully consistent updated state, and the parent must be recovered completely.
feedback.details: Undo must mirror all state changes, not only the visible assignment.

items.prompt: Arrange failure handling for a branch that finds no valid completion.

elements.text: Receive failure from the child search.
Restore all state changed by the candidate.
Try the next legal candidate.
Return failure only after every sibling also fails.

feedback.reason: One failed branch is not a failed parent until all legal alternatives are exhausted.
feedback.details: Restoration must occur before the next sibling is tested.

items.prompt: Arrange a find-one success path when the board must be restored before return.

elements.text: A recursive child reports success.
Undo the current branch's board and path mutations.
Return success to the caller.
Each caller restores its own branch mutation while unwinding.

feedback.reason: Success propagation and restoration interleave during stack unwinding.
feedback.details: Every stack frame owns and reverses the mutation it introduced.

items.prompt: A depth-d solver copies a path of length proportional to the current depth for every recursive child. Along one active root-to-leaf chain, select an upper bound on simultaneously retained copied-path space.
constraints: At depth i, a distinct copied path of length O(i) remains active.

feedback.reason: Active copied paths can total 1 + 2 + ... + d elements along the recursion chain.
feedback.details: In-place choose-recurse-unchoose can reduce active path storage to O(d), at the cost of requiring correct restoration.
